preamble: The fortition of a voiced retroflex flap [ɽ] into a voiced retroflex stop [ɖ]. This strengthening process increases articulatory stability, often in word-initial or stressed positions.

examples.note: Dialectal fortition or hypercorrection where flaps are realized as full stops.
Restoration of historical stops in dialects that previously flapped rhotics.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Kristoffersen, G. (2000). The Phonology of Norwegian.
